questions.question: A user wants to rename multiple files in a folder on Windows. What is the correct approach to do this efficiently?
questions.option_1: Drag files to another folder and rename them there
questions.option_2: Open each file and save it with a new name
questions.option_3: Select all files → Right-click → Rename → Type a base name
questions.option_4: Press Ctrl + Alt + R to rename all files

questions.question: Which of the following actions will move a file to the Recycle Bin in Windows?
questions.option_3: Press Enter on the selected file

questions.question: Which of the following pairs is correctly matched with both its abbreviation and its function?
questions.option_1: PCB – Printed Chip Base – Transfers data from CPU to display
questions.option_2: CPU – Central Processing Unit – Stores user files permanently
questions.option_3: RAM – Read Access Memory – Runs the operating system even after power is off
questions.option_4: ROM – Read Only Memory – Stores boot instructions needed when the computer is powered on

questions.question: When renaming a file or folder in MS Windows, what happens if a user changes the file extension (e.g. from .txt to .doc)?
questions.option_1: The file is converted into a different program's file format.
questions.option_2: A warning is displayed to confirm the change in extension.
questions.option_3: The file will no longer open or be accessible.
questions.option_4: The file's format is changed automatically to match the new extension.

questions.question: Why is it important to organize files and folders?
questions.option_1: To make the computer run faster
questions.option_2: To easily locate and access files

questions.question: Which key combination is used to permanently delete a file without sending it to the Recycle Bin?
After deleting a file from a folder, a user realises that it was an important file. Which of the following steps should they follow to restore it?
questions.option_1: Press Ctrl + Z repeatedly until it reappears
questions.option_2: Open Recycle Bin → Right-click the file → Click Restore
questions.option_3: Restart the computer and the file will return
questions.option_4: Use Alt + Tab to recover the file

questions.question: To adjust the screen resolution or change the desktop background in Windows, which of the following would a user typically access?
Which of the following is true for Read-Only Memory (ROM)?
questions.option_1: It stores data that can be modified by the user.
questions.option_2: It is volatile memory that stores data temporarily.
questions.option_4: It stores the booting instructions permanently.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to access programs, files and settings in Windows 7?
Which of the following applications can help you NOT to create a file?
Which of the following is an example of secondary memory?
The primary purpose of the cache memory is to:
questions.option_3: increase the speed of data retrieval
